description: This conceptual diagram illustrates the geometric observation setup involving a Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) Platform, a Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) Platform, and a ground-based GNSS Station. The SAR Platform is depicted on the upper left, transmitting a signal (thin blue line) towards the GNSS Station on the ground. The GNSS Platform is shown on the upper right, transmitting its signal (thin blue line) also towards the GNSS Station. A dashed black line connects a point on the SAR signal path to a point on the GNSS signal path. Two thicker blue lines originate from the GNSS Station and extend along the paths towards the SAR and GNSS platforms, representing the observed baselines or measurements. A label "(b)" is present in the bottom right corner.
